checks.description: After driving a boundary freq value, wave_out must eventually assume a defined binary value (0 or 1) and remain stable until the next toggle.
notes: Conservative guardrail: does not assume exact cycle count for the toggle.
checks.pass_condition: wave_out transitions from unknown/X to a defined 0 or 1 within a bounded but unspecified number of clock cycles and does not revert to X before the next observed toggle.
checks.trigger_condition: When the plan drives freq to its minimum (0) or maximum (255) value.

checks.description: Observe that two successive legal freq drives produce at least two distinct wave_out toggle events, preserving order.
notes: No exact latency is required; only that two observable toggles occur in the same order as the two drives.
checks.pass_condition: After the first freq drive, wave_out toggles at least once; after the second drive, a subsequent toggle occurs, and the second toggle is observed after the first.
checks.trigger_condition: When the plan applies two back-to-back freq values with minimal idle spacing.

checks.description: Changing freq must eventually affect the observed toggle period of wave_out.
notes: The check does not require a precise period measurement; it only requires that after a freq change, the interval between two successive toggles differs from the interval observed before the change.
checks.pass_condition: Measure two successive toggle intervals (in clock cycles) before and after the freq change; the intervals are not equal, indicating frequency adaptation.
checks.trigger_condition: When the plan drives an initial freq, observes toggles, then drives a different freq value.
